observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

a.) the GSO to:

Spearhead the conduct of semestral (or oftener, depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items and report the results thereof in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and submit the report to the AT; and
 
Prepare the SSMI based on said physical inventory count and forward the said report to the MA as her basis in recognition of supplies expense; and


b.) the MA to recognize the expense in the books based on the said SSMI and not on estimates.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO-Designate has conducted the required semestral physical inventory count of items.



The GSO-Designate has initiated steps to prepare the SSMI based on the inventory count and committed to submit the same to the MA.


The MA monitors inventory balances and their usage using available records and continues to coordinate with the GSO-Designate for needed reports.
observations.reason: Although a physical count had already been conducted, SSMI was not prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

a.) the GSO to:

Spearhead the conduct of semestral (or oftener, depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items and report the results thereof in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and submit the report to the AT; and
 
Prepare the SSMI based on said physical inventory count and forward the said report to the MA as her basis in recognition of supplies expense; and


b.) the MA to recognize the expense in the books based on the said SSMI and not on estimates.

The GSO-Designate has conducted the required semestral physical inventory count of items.



The GSO-Designate has initiated steps to prepare the SSMI based on the inventory count and committed to submit the same to the MA.


The MA monitors inventory balances and their usage using available records and continues to coordinate with the GSO-Designate for needed reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the GFPS TWG to:

Leverage the available tools to strengthen gender analysis and gender assessment to facilitate effective identification of PPAs to be included under the GAD fund;



Increase the number of organization-focused PPAs intended to capacitate the GFPS and the rest of the implementing offices on how to undertake gender analysis in the GPB and implement the same; and



Institutionalize the LGU GAD database so gender issues in the Municipality can be more readily determined and addressed.
observations.managementAction:
The GFPS TWG acknowledged the need to enhance gender analysis and is currently exploring the use of appropriate tools and approaches to improve PPA identification.





The LGU conducted capacity-building activities for GFPS members and implementing offices to improve understanding and application of gender analysis in GAD planning.



Efforts to establish a comprehensive GAD database are still underway, with the GFPS TWG gathering relevant sex-disaggregated data from departments.
observations.reason:
Post-audit and inquiry with Management disclosed that certain PPAs, namely the Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS) and procurement of medicines, still lacked gender analysis and were not clearly anchored on identified gender issues.

Reiterated in Observation No. 7, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Development and Planning Officer to: 

Prioritize programs, projects, and activities that will benefit registered tobacco farmers in line with the road map of the tobacco industry;



Conduct public consultations or assemblies with tobacco farmers duly identified by the NTA to determine their current needs; and



Allocate at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the total share for cooperative programs, livelihood projects, and financial support for registered tobacco farmers.
observations.managementAction:
The MPDO acknowledged the observation and noted that the matter will be taken into account in the planning of future programs. 





Initial efforts were made by the LGU, through the Mayor and concerned offices, to coordinate with identified tobacco farmers for possible consultations on their current needs.


A total of ₱44,491,454.00 was appropriated under the RA 7171 and RA 8240 funds, of which ₱992,483.00 was allocated for the procurement of farm supplies intended to support local farmers, while a total of ₱38,198,393.00 was allotted for the implementation of 11 FMR projects.
observations.reason:
Review of the SAAOB and related supplemental budgets disclosed that only ₱992,483.00, or approximately 2.53% of the appropriated ₱44,491,454.00 RA 7171 and 8240 funds, was allocated for cooperative, livelihood, or financial support projects 
which falls significantly short of the recommended 25% minimum allocation for such programs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to expedite the procurement of the equipment and machinery necessary for the accreditation and operation of the meat establishment. 



Additionally, to seek the assistance of the NMIS on the evaluation of their slaughterhouse to assure the public that it is operating within the minimum requirements for facilities, equipment, and hygienic operational procedures for slaughtering food animals.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU acknowledged the recommendation but has yet to undertake full procurement and coordination efforts necessary for the accreditation of the slaughterhouse.


Management stated that they are already starting to implement the necessary procedures, such as facilitating the required visits by the ARD of the NMIS, for the slaughterhouse accreditation and for expanding the reach of the slaughterhouse like catering to the needs of different LGUs.
observations.reason: The AT’s validation of PPE disclosed that the slaughterhouse remains non-operational, with no significant action yet taken toward procuring the necessary equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the following:
 
The BAC and Municipal Engineer (ME) to immediately submit the lacking documents for the 32 infrastructure projects to ensure procedural compliance and facilitate inspection; and

The MA, ME and BAC to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements before the payment of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned offices have yet to comply with the submission of the lacking documents for the 32 infrastructure projects.


Documentary requirements are being submitted during processing of disbursement vouchers regarding these transactions.
observations.reason: The lacking documents have not yet been received by the AT as of date.

observations.recommendation:
To ensure optimal appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF, we recommended that the LCE direct the LDRRM Officer and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to:

Monitor regularly the utilization of the fund, evaluate the reasons for the non-implementation, and ascertain the present viability of the PPAs; otherwise, realign or reprogram the funds for more important initiatives.




Prioritize the implementation of PPAs under Continuing Appropriations. In the event of discontinuance, the PPAs may be reprogrammed for more relevant projects, and



Judiciously plan the use of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from previous years, which were transferred to the special trust fund, by including under a separate section in the LDRRMFIP the list of priority projects and activities to be charged out of the said funds.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO has exerted efforts to ensure efficient fund utilization by initiating measures to support the timely implementation of programs, projects, and activities under the 70% LDRRMF.





The concerned offices are currently reviewing PPAs under Continuing Appropriations as part of the annual investment planning process to determine their viability for implementation or possible reprogramming.


The LGU continues to assess the appropriate use of unexpended balances from previous years.
observations.reason: Review of the financial statements disclosed that several PPAs under Continuing Appropriations of the DRRMF from CYs 2019 to 2022 remain neither implemented nor re-programmed as of date.

The ME to:

Provide the MA the sub-components and related cost of each completed Public Infrastructure projects; and

observations.recommendation:

Convene with the department heads to identify the source agencies of the different funds which remained unliquidated since CY 2017 and 2018, and thereafter make representations to the concerned NGAs or LGUs for assistance in the liquidation of the said funds.



Direct the assigned department head to prepare the necessary documents for the liquidation and furnish the MA for the proper recording of the transaction in the books of accounts.

The assigned department to prepare the necessary documents for the liquidation and consequently furnish the MA for the proper recording in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to properly schedule the implementation of PPAs by assigning specific dates or shorter time frames with due consideration on the climate of the country.

Reiterated in Observation No. 8, Part II of this report.

The Municipal Assessor to update his ledgers to confirm with the records of MT unless he was able to provide details of its Report on Real Property Assessment which thereafter, requires reconciliation.

The MA, MT, and Municipal Assessor to reconcile their respective records of PPE – Land, Buildings and Other Structures.


The MT and Accountant to create and maintain property cards and subsidiary ledger cards for all items of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):

To include in their post-qualification a comparative evaluation showing the number of projects with the same equipment and key personnel pledged and assigned by the same lowest calculated bidder for different projects with simultaneous or overlapping implementation period.





To formulate policies on the sufficiency and availability of equipment pledged by the contractors with regard to projects simultaneously awarded to the same contractors to avoid delay in project implementation.



To extend comparison with other government agencies before declaring the bids as responsive.



To set limits for the number of contracts to be handled by key personnel, in order to ensure reasonableness of performance without sacrificing quality and timeliness of project completion.
observations.managementAction:
The TWG conducted post-qualification for infrastructure projects under bid.





The BAC was not able to formulate policies on the sufficiency and availability of equipment pledged by the contractors with regard to projects simultaneously awarded to the same contractors.



The BAC declared the lowest calculated bidder as responsive based on the report of the TWG. 


BAC required the submission of Manpower Schedule and Key Personnel as attached to technical documents.
observations.reason:
Post-qualification report does not include number of projects with the same equipment and key personnel pledged and assigned by the same lowest calculated bidder for different projects with simultaneous or overlapping implementation period. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, Part II of this report.

Not Implemented.
Policies were not formulated on the sufficiency and availability of equipment pledged by the contractors with regard to projects simultaneously awarded to the same contractors.
Reiterated in Observation No. 9, Part II of this report.

Not Implemented.
No additional procedures were adopted to check the correctness and reasonableness of bids  prior to declaring the bids as responsive

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, Part II of this report.

Not Implemented.
Although the submission of Manpower Schedule and Key Personnel requirements was enforced, no specific limits were set to regulate the number of contracts assigned to key personnel.

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that the LCE:
Require regular reconciliation among the Municipal Assessor, MT, and MA, setting a clear schedule and assigning focal persons from each office to coordinate on RPT data; and
Strengthen record management and inter-office coordination by directing the adoption of appropriate systems—such as shared digital records, spreadsheet databases, or other reliable tracking tools—to ensure consistent documentation and effective information sharing among the concerned offices.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the:
MT to assess the current staffing capacity of the office and, if needed, coordinate with the LCE to request additional personnel or support. Thereafter, facilitate the timely updating and maintenance of complete delinquency records—including the RPTAR and Taxpayer’s Index Cards—using actual taxpayer-level data and payment records that reflect the status of all collectible RPT and SET;
MA to revisit and adjust the reported RPT and SET receivables in the financial statements after the Treasurer’s delinquency records have been fully updated, reconciled, and certified, to ensure that the recorded balances are properly supported and represent accurate and enforceable claims. Moving forward, maintain close coordination with the Treasurer’s Office to ensure alignment in future reporting periods; and
Municipal Assessor to regularly update the assessment roll to reflect changes in property ownership, classification, and valuation, and ensure that the updated data is made available for use in levy computation and reconciliation with the Treasurer’s and Accountant’s records.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require that the budget allocation for TA under her Office be reviewed and adjusted accordingly, in compliance with existing government regulations prohibiting the simultaneous receipt of TA and the use of an official government vehicle;
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and MA to coordinate to ensure that the appropriation for TA is either reallocated or withheld, as appropriate, to prevent the risk of double compensation and to uphold the principles of fiscal responsibility and regularity in disbursements; and
Direct the MA to ensure that all reimbursements for fuel consumption are properly recorded using the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses account.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to:
Ensure that all COS employees execute and submit a sworn declaration and;
Monitor the deduction of withheld taxes in the payroll.
MA to determine and verify the correct amount of taxes due and ensure the timely remittance of all taxes withheld.

We recommended that the MA and the MT develop internal policies and procedures for the evaluation and approval of employees’ salary loan applications, and submit these policies to the LCE for approval.

We therefore recommended that the LCE direct the GAD Focal Point System to:
Facilitate the update and maintenance of the GAD Database using sex-disaggregated data from the CBMS and other relevant sources, in compliance with Annex B and Section C.2 of the Joint Circular, to ensure that identified gender issues are evidence-based and reflective of actual conditions;
Institutionalize a more rigorous evaluation process for proposed GAD PPAs by requiring the use of gender analysis tools such as the HGDG and ensuring alignment with documented gender issues, as required under Sections C.1.4 and C.4 of the Circular. Proposals that do not address gender gaps should be revised or excluded from the GAD Plan, and
Strengthen the functionality of the GFPS by providing adequate support and capacity-building, and by establishing internal mechanisms to harmonize interdepartmental proposals with GAD mandates—ensuring that only compliant and gender-responsive programs are included in the final Plan.

We therefore recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to:
Strategically schedule and monitor the implementation of PPAs under Disaster Prevention and Mitigation and Disaster Preparedness to ensure that these are conducted well ahead of high-risk periods in line with the proactive intent of JMC No. 2013-1; and
Ensure that planned PPAs, particularly those involving procurement of essential goods, equipment, and infrastructure, are implemented on time to maximize their impact on community safety and disaster readiness.
We further recommended that the LCE, in coordination with the MDRRMO and the HRMO, conduct a comprehensive review of the current staffing structure and workflow of the DRRM Office.   As warranted, consideration should be given to reassigning additional personnel or providing the necessary technical support, capacity-building activities, and process improvement measures to strengthen the timely planning, procurement, and implementation of DRRM-related PPAs.

We recommended that the HoPE:
Through its BAC Secretariat, meticulously review the chronology of documents prior to the finalization and signing of the contract agreement to avoid prematurely committing to the winning bidder. Additionally, upon acceptance of the NOA by the winning bidder, a written acknowledgment of the receipt date should be provided to properly track the timeline, in accordance with the prescribed maximum calendar days set forth in RA 9184 and its IRR;
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to thoroughly review the validity and legality of the submitted performance security by the Lowest Calculated Responsive Bid (LCRB)/Single Calculated Responsive Bidder (SCRB) before accepting it and attaching it to the contract.  Any alterations to this indispensable document should be grounds for the revocation of the award, in order to safeguard government resources in the event of non-fulfillment of obligations; and
Direct the BAC to remind prospective bidders during the pre-bid conference about the timeliness and completeness of information in the required Class “A” documents, particularly, of declared ongoing and awarded but not yet started government and private contracts, and the accuracy of declared NFCC. Failure to comply should result in the disqualification of non-compliant bidders during the bid evaluation or post qualification, in accordance with the grounds set under Section 30.1 and Section 34 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC, together with its TWG, to:
Thoroughly conduct the post-qualification of the LCRB to ensure that required personnel for the contract are available and its manpower is not compromised; and
Remind prospective bidders to update their financial and technical documents prior to participating in future bids.

We therefore recommended that the LCE direct the LSB, in coordination with the MA, to:
Submit the lacking inspection reports, acknowledgment receipts, and completion certificates to substantiate the validity and propriety of the affected disbursements, and
In the ensuing years, ensure that all transactions funded under the SEF are supported by complete and accurate documentation, in compliance with the relevant provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the LCE direct the LSB, in coordination with the MA, to ensure the proper and timely transmittal of the SEF Budget to the DepEd Central Office, Sangguniang Bayan (SB), and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG as prescribed in DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We therefore recommended that the LCE direct the MENRO to:
Strengthen fund utilization planning by identifying compliance-driven priorities early in the budget cycle and aligning them with available resources, to promote proactive, safe, and standards-based implementation of the Solid Waste Management Program;
Strategically incorporate the procurement and distribution of PPEs into the annual Solid Waste Management Fund programming, ensuring that all collectors and personnel directly involved in the handling of solid waste are adequately equipped in accordance with Section 23 (a) of RA No. 9003 and its IRR; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Health Officer or other appropriate agencies to facilitate the conduct of training programs aimed at enhancing the safety awareness and technical competencies of garbage collectors and utility workers. These may include, but are not limited to, TESDA-accredited courses such as Garbage Collector NC I, in compliance with Section 23 (b) of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MPDO to:
Allocate at least 25% of the total share, or consider increasing the allocation of RA 7171 and RA 8240 funds for cooperative programs, livelihood initiatives, and financial assistance benefiting registered tobacco farmers; and
Ensure strict adherence to the provisions of the applicable LBM governing fund utilization, particularly with respect to the receipt and proper use of funds from RA 7171 and RA 8240.

We recommended that the LCE continue to implement and enforce the use of the ATM Payroll System for the payment of all salaries, wages and other benefits of employees, including job order personnel, in accordance with Section 3 of EO No. 170 s. 2022 to promote operational efficiency and strengthen internal controls over payroll system management.

We therefore recommend that the LCE direct the MT to:
Improve the RPT collection process by setting up a clear and regular follow-up system—such as sending reminder letters beyond the usual schedules, working closely with barangay officials to reach out to taxpayers, and visiting areas where many accounts remain unpaid. The Treasurer should also track taxpayer responses to take timely action; and
Adopt community-based approaches to encourage voluntary payment of taxes, such as providing simple recognition or incentives to early or full payers during barangay assemblies or community events. While legal remedies such as levy or public auction may still be pursued, these should be considered only after all reasonable and locally accepted efforts to secure voluntary compliance have been exhausted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Health Office and the Accounting Office to closely and regularly coordinate with PhilHealth.





Moreover, we recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to facilitate the timely reconciliation of benefit claims
observations.managementAction:
The MHO and Accounting Office have started regular coordination with PhilHealth to address benefit claim issues.





The MA, in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer, has initiated reconciliation efforts with PhilHealth to ensure timely recording of claims.
observations.reason: Validated that the MA, in coordination with the MHO, has begun reconciling PhilHealth claims, as evidenced by updated schedule shown to the Audit Team (AT) during inquiry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) ensure that the SEF Budget of the Municipality is presented by program, projects, and activity (PPA), by allotment class and per school using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 in the ensuing years and that neither lump-sum appropriations nor appropriations with generic terms are included in the said budget.
observations.managementAction: The LSB to reflect specific allocations per school to address the observation.
observations.reason: Review of the SEF budget disclosed that the PPAs were already allocated per school.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LSB comply with the requirements for hiring additional teachers using the LSB funds by seeking approval from the DepEd Central Office and providing justification for hiring teachers as provided by DepEd Memorandum No. OR-GO-2016-GOA-0700, dated June 14, 2016.


We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the MA to process disbursement vouchers only when the corresponding accomplishment reports by the locally hired teachers are furnished to her.
observations.managementAction:
The LSB has adopted internal measures to ensure compliance with DepEd and COA requirements, including the preparation of justification letters and gathering of supporting documents for teacher hiring.



The MA now requires locally hired teachers to submit accomplishment reports prior to processing their salaries.
observations.reason:
The LSB submitted a justification letter regarding the teacher hiring and committed to process the necessary documents in compliance with DepEd and COA guidelines.

Implemented.
Previously missing accomplishment reports have been submitted and subsequent disbursement vouchers already included the required attachments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

a.) the GSO to:

Spearhead the conduct of semestral (or oftener, depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items and report the results thereof in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and submit the report to the AT; and
 
Prepare the SSMI based on said physical inventory count and forward the said report to the MA as her basis in recognition of supplies expense; and


b.) the MA to recognize the expense in the books based on the said SSMI and not on estimates.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO-Designate has conducted the required semestral physical inventory count of items.



The GSO-Designate has initiated steps to prepare the SSMI based on the inventory count and committed to submit the same to the MA.


The MA monitors inventory balances and their usage using available records and continues to coordinate with the GSO-Designate for needed reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU establish controls to ensure that source documents, particularly Purchase Requests (PRs), Purchase Orders (POs), and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs), are properly filled out and contain all required information in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs to improve the efficiency and transparency of and compliance to the procurement process and reduce the risk of errors or discrepancies in its financial records.
observations.managementAction: Following the exit conference, the MA initiated measures to ensure that end-user departments comply with the required fields in PRs, POs, and AIRs.
observations.reason: Post-audit of related transactions disclosed that the required fields in PRs, POs, and AIRs for procurements under the SVP were already properly accomplished and complied with.

observations.recommendation:
To ensure optimal appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF, we recommended that the LCE direct the LDRRM Officer and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to:

Monitor regularly the utilization of the fund, evaluate the reasons for the non-implementation, and ascertain the present viability of the PPAs; otherwise, realign or reprogram the funds for more important initiatives.




Prioritize the implementation of PPAs under Continuing Appropriations. In the event of discontinuance, the PPAs may be reprogrammed for more relevant projects, and



Judiciously plan the use of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from previous years, which were transferred to the special trust fund, by including under a separate section in the LDRRMFIP the list of priority projects and activities to be charged out of the said funds.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO has exerted efforts to ensure efficient fund utilization by initiating measures to support the timely implementation of programs, projects, and activities under the 70% LDRRMF.





The concerned offices are currently reviewing PPAs under Continuing Appropriations as part of the annual investment planning process to determine their viability for implementation or possible reprogramming.


The LGU continues to assess the appropriate use of unexpended balances from previous years.
observations.reason: Review of transactions disclosed that ₱3,958,375.18 out of ₱5,018,770.33 under the 70% DRRM Fund was utilized, with 15 out of 17-line items in the DRRM budget having recorded disbursements, reflecting active management efforts in optimizing the use of available resources.

observations.recommendation: 1.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health Office and the Accounting Office to closely and regularly coordinate with PhilHealth. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the timely reconciliation of benefit claims.

2.6. We recommended that the Local School Board ensure that the SEF Budget of the Municipality is presented by program, projects, and activity (PPA), by allotment class and per school using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 in the ensuing years and that neither lump-sum appropriations nor appropriations with generic terms are included in the said budget.

3.9. We recommended that the Local School Board comply with the requirements for hiring additional teachers using the LSB funds by seeking approval from the DepEd Central Office and providing justification for hiring teachers as provided by DepEd Memorandum No. OR-GO-2016-GOA-0700, dated June 14, 2016.
3.10. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to process disbursement vouchers only when the corresponding accomplishment reports by the locally hired teachers are furnished to her.

4.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a.) the GSO to:
. Spearhead the conduct of semestral (or oftener, depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items and report the results thereof in the RPCI, and submit the report to the Audit Team; and
. Prepare the SSMI based on said physical inventory count and forward the said report to the Municipal Accountant as her basis in recognition of supplies expense; and
b.) the Municipal Accountant to recognize the expense in the books based on the said SSMI and not on estimates.

5.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GFPS TWG to:
a. Leverage the available tools to strengthen gender analysis and gender assessment to facilitate effective identification of PPAs to be included under the GAD fund;
b. Increase the number of organization-focused PPAs intended to capacitate the GFPS and the rest of the implementing offices on how to undertake gender analysis in the GPB and implement the same; and
c. Institutionalize the LGU GAD database so gender issues in the Municipality can be more readily determined and addressed.

6.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development and Planning Officer to:
a. Prioritize programs, projects, and activities that will benefit registered tobacco farmers in line with the road map of the tobacco industry;
b. Conduct public consultations or assemblies with tobacco farmers duly identified by the NTA to determine their current needs; and
c. Allocate at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the total share for cooperative programs, livelihood projects, and financial support for registered tobacco farmers.

7.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to expedite the procurement of the equipment and machinery necessary for the accreditation and operation of the meat establishment. Additionally, to seek the assistance of the NMIS on the evaluation of their slaughterhouse to assure the public that it is operating within the minimum requirements for facilities, equipment and hygienic operational procedures for slaughtering food animals.

8.7. With the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
a. The BAC and Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the lacking documents for the 32 infrastructure projects to ensure procedural compliance and facilitate inspection; and
b. The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements before the payment of infrastructure projects.

9.13. We recommended that the LGU establish controls to ensure that source documents, particularly Purchase Requests (PRs), Purchase Orders (POs), and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs), are properly filled out and contain all required information in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs to improve the efficiency and transparency of and compliance to the procurement process and reduce the risk of errors or discrepancies in its financial records.

Designate a GSO other than the Municipal Treasurer to relieve the latter of additional duties and help fulfill the tasks enumerated herein and assign a Property Inspector to conduct inspection of deliveries to the Municipality.

Direct the assigned department head to prepare the necessary documents for the liquidation and furnish the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of the transaction in the books of accounts.

TWG to conduct Bid Evaluation and Post Qualification diligently, taking advantage of the maximum time period set in accordance with pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR; and
Bids and Awards Committee to:
Remind prospective bidders during the pre-bid conference on the completeness of information in the required Class “A” documents, particularly, the completeness of the declared ongoing and awarded but not yet started government and private contracts and accuracy of the declared NFCC. Otherwise, disqualify non-compliant bidders during bid evaluation or post qualification based on the grounds set under Section 30.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Secure the additional requirements from the winning bidder before the signing of contract in accordance with Section 37.2 of the2016 revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the departments
or offices to submit detailed PPAs pertaining to the funds allotted to them
as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer for the proper classification of
accounts in the preparation of the annual budget.

1.10. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that PPAs are assigned with proper accounts before finalizing the
annual budget of the Municipality.

2.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a. Require the Collecting Officers to remit all their undeposited
collections to him at the end of the day;
b. Procure an office vault or safe for cash, with combination and key
lock to be used as storage for undeposited collections;
c. Modify the policy on having December 24 as the last day of deposit
for the year’s collections, and set it to until the last banking day of the
year; and
d. Deposit intact all his collections as well as all collections turned over
to him by the collectors daily or not later than the next banking day to
decrease the risk of loss from misappropriation or theft.

3.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer, who is also the BAC Chairperson, to
ensure that all priority programs, projects, and activities which are
considered to be necessary be included in the Annual Investment Plan and
subsequently, in the Annual Procurement Plan with the appropriate
procurement methods, and to consolidate projects which are similar in
nature into one procurement and require that all procurements be
undertaken through competitive bidding unless it meets all the conditions
under alternative modes of procurement.

4.7. In order to ensure the quality of the infrastructures and efficiency of the
project implementation, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
direct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to inspect the materials delivered for projects to
ensure their completeness before their delivery to requesting barangays;
b. The Municipal Engineer to submit POWs and Detailed Estimates of all
infrastructure projects under alternative mode of procurement, together
with the schedule of payment of labor component, if any, for further
verification, and,
c. The Management likewise to submit Inspection Reports as well as
Certificates of Completion in order to ascertain completion of projects.

4.8. Further, we recommended that all infrastructure projects in the ensuing
years, be supported with POWs with labor program and Detailed Cost
Estimates.

5.15. To facilitate the effective conduct of public bidding and in-depth
assessment and evaluation of its bidders, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman to adopt the 6th Edition of
PBD in all of the procurement undertakings of the LGU and ensure that:
a. All provisions in the Bid Form, Omnibus Sworn Statement, Contract
Agreement are included;
b. The details and distinctive requirements of each project in the Special
Conditions of the Contract, Specifications, Bill of Quantities are
specified; and
c. The Checklist of Technical and Financial Documents used in declaring
compliance of bidders during opening of bids is complete.

Give priority to officials and employees for which the budgeted amount of monetization of leave credits was intended. The grant of monetization of more than 50% should only be made if there were savings from the budgeted amount; and

Grant payment of terminal leave benefits only after severance from office
observations.managementAction:

observations.reason:
This CY 2020, monetization of leave credits granted by the LGU amounted to  ₱689,708.70   and retention of five days leave credits of each employee were complied.


Implemented.

An appropriation of ₱2,162,000.00 was allotted by the LGU and it had settled a total amount of ₱2,158,754.63. As at year-end, the LGU has no pending claims for terminal benefits.


Implemented.

The appropriated amount for terminal leave benefits was granted to employees and officials who were separated from the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Head of Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct the in-depth technical and financial review of the documents submitted by the bidders during post-qualification by verifying, validating and ascertaining that the lowest calculated responsive bidder has the technical capacity to simultaneously implement all projects to be awarded.  In cases there would be simultaneous or overlapping projects to be awarded to the same contractor, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to require the contractor to augment its key personnel and equipment to be committed to the project.  Otherwise, disqualify future bids whose key personnel and equipment are already pledged/ used in other ongoing projects. 

Also, the Municipal Engineer to monitor simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work.

The quality of implementation of projects may be again compromised due to insufficiency of equipment and personnel.

For strict compliance.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that Management:
Reinforce and strengthen the function of the Physical Inventory Committee.
Conduct periodic physical count of PPE and prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team.
Strictly adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines on property management particularly Section 114 and 124 of the MNGAS. Prepare and maintain appropriate property cards and property ledger cards to effectively identify and monitor the LGUs PPE.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that Management require the Head of the Requesting Unit and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare Obligation Request upon payment including those cash advances and such form shall be an integral part of Disbursement Voucher. Further, the Municipal Accountant to obligate the said cash advances or advances to contractors and the Budget Officer to update its Registries and the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations to reflect the unrecorded cash advances or advances to contractors.

We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using collection to accommodate employees’ checks and other related transactions and to comply strictly with the provisions on deposit of collections which shall be done intact and on a daily basis. We further recommended that collections on hand be deposited immediately.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to: 

Reconcile the records of PPE - Land and Buildings accounts between the Accounting Office, Treasurer’s Office and Assessor’s Office. 

Record the lots and buildings at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction, based on available supporting documents. 

Establish or re-organize the Physical Inventory Committee and ensure that they perform their duties and responsibilities to properly account for the PPE owned by the LGU.

Submit a Report on the Physical Count of PPE.

Ensure that property cards and subsidiary ledger cards are created and maintained in the Treasurer’s and Accounting offices, respectively, for all items of PPE.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE issued E.O. No. 225-A, s. 2019 to re-organize the Municipal Inventory Committee, and an employee was already assigned to maintain property cards, and other records.

Likewise, Treasury and Accounting Offices have collated the listing of LGU’s buildings and lots owned.
observations.reason:
Physical Inventory Committee has been re-organized through the issuance of an Executive Order, however, the committee has not been able to fully conduct physical inventory due to voluminous workload in their regular work/function. As a result, no Physical Inventory Report was finalized and submitted. 

The Treasury also provided listing of the lots and buildings reconciled with the Assessor’s Office records, however, total costs reflected in the books cannot be specifically identified to individual items of property, due to insufficient documents as basis for the cost breakdown of the lump-sum amounts. 

Property cards and subsidiary ledger cards are not yet in place, although Management assured that they will start transferring the property listing into the appropriate property cards and ledger cards, accordingly.

Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory
of all its properties and thereafter, prepare and submit the RCPPE
for adjustment in the agency books or property records, as necessary.
2. Assign personnel at Treasury Office to maintain property cards and
keep inventory records updated.
3. Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
respectively to reconcile regularly the property cards maintained at
the Treasury Office and ledger cards of Accounting Office to
ascertain the existence, validity and accuracy of the PPE accounts.

We have recommended that the municipal government allocate funds
for insurance of the municipal building with the GSIS in order to mitigate
the risks in case of damage of property caused by fortuitous events.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the ICO-
Municipal Treasurer to formulate strategies to intensify tax collections due
the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services
and facilities and avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 RA 7160. We
further recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer prepare the
Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies to include all tax
delinquents and reconcile the same with the accounting records.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the
concerned officials to match the PPAs charged to the 20% MDF with
available financing resources in conformity with Paragraph 8.6 of DILG-
NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 Series of 2007.

We have therefore, recommended that the Local Chief Executive
enjoin the concerned officials and employees on the following:
1. Observe one-hour breaks for breakfast, lunch, or supper and rest, and
every 3 hours of continuous overtime service, or as may be necessary.
2. No employee be allowed to render overnight service for more than two
consecutive nights for health reasons and to ensure employee
productivity.
3. Accomplish properly the Daily Time Records as to time-in and out of
regular work hours indicating also the leaves of absences, official travels,
etc., if applicable.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the
following:
1. Comply with the documentary requirements of procurement and see
to it that such are attached to Disbursement Vouchers.
2. Period of procurement (both latest and earliest possible time) be
followed as provided in Annex C of the Revised IRR so as not to
sacrifice effectiveness and efficiency of procurement if made earlier
and to prevent delay in the implementation of projects if made later.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring entity direct
the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Project Monitoring Report
approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of
the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
